reforms.description: The Coalition Agreement has announced an increase in spending on public support for R&D of up to EUR 400 million per year by 2020, aimed at strengthening innovation performance. Additionally, the government plans to broaden access to academic research and increase the share of direct innovation grants to SMEs, enhancing their capacity for R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D and support for SMEs enhances innovation capacity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been announced in the Coalition Agreement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant increase in public R&D funding, which is a structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The Coalition Agreement has announced an increase in spending on public support for R&D of up to EUR 400 million (0.05% of GDP) per year by 2020.

reforms.description: The government plans to provide EUR 100 million of additional funding on research infrastructure in 2018 and 2019 to support innovation and R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investment in research infrastructure supports R&D activities, leading to enhanced innovation capabilities.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been announced by the government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding increase is significant but not as transformative as the overall R&D funding increase.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to provide EUR 100 million (0.01% of GDP) of additional funding on research infrastructure in 2018 and 2019.

reforms.description: The government has announced an increase in the effective tax rate on earnings related to intangible assets under the innovation box tax, raising it from 5% to 7%. Additionally, the Coalition Agreement has proposed reducing the tax incentives for self-employed individuals, aiming to ensure they build adequate savings for disability and ageing risks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: While the increase in the tax rate may reduce incentives for some firms, the overall aim is to ensure a more sustainable tax system.
reforms.status_evidence: The changes have been announced in the Coalition Agreement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The adjustments are significant but do not represent a major overhaul of the tax system.
reforms.source_quote: The effective tax rate on earnings related to intangible assets will be increased from 5% to 7%.

reforms.description: The government announced plans to introduce individual learning accounts to enhance lifelong learning, particularly targeting low-skilled individuals who face barriers to education and training. Additionally, the government pledged to bolster the funds to the Public Employment Service (PES) to provide targeted personal coaching for older workers, linking eligibility for support to the official retirement age.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing lifelong learning and targeted support for older workers improves the skill set of the workforce, contributing to innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans have been announced by the government.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiatives are significant for improving human capital but do not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: The government announced in the Coalition Agreement their plans to introduce individual learning accounts.
